title: Continuous Delivery Outcomes, Flow, Feedback, and Evidence · Automation Case: Ci Ci Cd Release Boundaries
summary: A civic TypeScript service ships manually, batches risky changes, measures only deployment count, and cannot connect a production incident to source, tests, artifact, approval, or rollback. Planned artifact: Add and verify the ci ci cd release boundaries increment in delivery outcome and evidence map. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Distinguish continuous integration, delivery, deployment, release, and feature exposure by their automation, decision, and stakeholder boundaries.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.config:
# cicd-github-actions cumulative delivery-system portfolio
# Browser labs perform deterministic workflow, policy, artifact, and release evidence review only.
# They never call GitHub APIs, start runners, execute learner commands, access Docker or registries, exchange OIDC tokens, or contact cloud services.
workspace: cicd
version-baseline:

  google-auth: 3
  deploy-cloudrun: 3
transfer-boundary: authorized-disposable-delivery-environment

content.title: Worked case pattern
content.text: A worked example service deploys 3 times per week but cannot connect reviewed source, required checks, immutable artifact, production revision, user signal, abort decision, or recovery. Define value and delivery evidence without gaming one metric. Competency probe: Distinguish continuous integration, delivery, deployment, release, and feature exposure by their automation, decision, and stakeholder boundaries. A defensible solution applies Ci Ci Cd Release Boundaries, preserves the governing invariant or value contract, and connects the observed result to delivery outcome and evidence map.

content.code:
# Worked focus: ci-ci-cd-release-boundaries — Distinguish continuous integration, delivery, deployment, release, and feature exposure by their automation, decision, and stakeholder boundaries.
  # Evidence: ci-worked-ci-delivery-outcomes-flow-evidence-5
  - competency: ci-worked-ci-delivery-outcomes-flow-evidence-5
    artifact: delivery-evidence.yaml
    decision: worked ci delivery outcomes flow evidence 5
    environment: single-job ephemeral self-hosted runner in a segmented subnet
    fault-injection: expire the only retained test artifact during incident reconstruction
    retained-proof: accessible redacted timeline with owner expiry and regression control
    case-key: worked5
    change: |
      hypothesis: safer-status-release
      source-revision: reviewed-sha
      checks: required-and-observed
      artifact: immutable-digest
      deployment: revision-identity
      user-signal: successful-status-read
      abort-signal: error-budget-breach
      recovery: prior-digest
    verify: review source-to-user evidence chain and calculate lead time failure and recovery boundaries
    failure: Reject deployment count as value, green checks as provenance, or pipeline completion as user outcome.
    repair: Link reviewed source, exact checks, immutable artifact, observed deployment, user signal, abort decision, and recovery evidence.
content.caption: Runnable CI/CD workflow, artifact, release, and transfer-evidence contract example; predict its result before running, then change one boundary input.

content.title: Non-worked case
content.text: Continuous delivery means every successful commit must immediately receive production traffic. This fails the Ci Ci Cd Release Boundaries contract because it omits a precondition, invariant, ownership rule, or counterexample needed by this exact case.
